intended_users: International students seeking guidance on how to access healthcare in Quebec.
service_description: If you are experiencing an emergency , a life-or limb-threatening situations or immediate safety concerns, call 911 or Go to the nearest hospital emergency department. You may also consult the Student Wellness Hub’s urgent care page to find an appropriate resource. Anyone living in Québec can also call Info-Santé, a free confidential telephone consultation service, at 811 to speak to a nurse 24/7.
delivery_context: Official navigation for on-campus, telehealth, and off-campus care routes.
contact_or_access_methods: Local Wellness Advisors: https://www.mcgill.ca/wellness-hub/about-hub/clinicians/local-wellness-advisors | book appointments: https://www.mcgill.ca/wellness-hub/get-support | 514-398-7992: tel:514-398-7992 | 514-329-1999: tel:514-329-1999
recommended_next_step: Review the appropriate care route; for emergencies call 911 or go to the nearest emergency department.
source_evidence_excerpt: If you are experiencing an emergency , a life-or limb-threatening situations or immediate safety concerns, call 911 or Go to the nearest hospital emergency department. You may also consult the Student Wellness Hub’s urgent care page to find an appropriate resource.
limitation: The navigator must not diagnose, recommend treatment, or determine whether symptoms are an emergency.

intended_users: International students exploring McGill scholarships, awards, financial aid, and related funding.
service_description: McGill recognises the challenges International students can face when studying away from home. Scholarships and Student Aid is committed to ensuring that qualified students from any geographic region are financially supported in their goals to enter and complete academic programs at the University. This page points to the various funding opportunities that will help support these goals. Newly admitted undergraduate students can apply to McGill’s Entrance Scholarship Program . In-course award programs for undergraduate students currently studying at McGill fall under individual faculty jurisdiction as do those for graduate students .
delivery_context: Online information covering multiple funding programs.
recommended_next_step: Review the funding categories and follow the official application route for the relevant program.
source_evidence_excerpt: McGill recognises the challenges International students can face when studying away from home. This page points to the various funding opportunities that will help support these goals.
limitation: Funding eligibility and application requirements vary by program and must be confirmed officially.

intended_users: McGill students seeking career advising, skills development, jobs, internships, and networking resources.
service_description: The Career Planning Service (CaPS), McGill’s central career services, supports student career and leadership development through career counselling and advising, skills development workshops, experiential learning, career exploration programs, mentoring, and career and networking events. CaPS provides and oversees a wide variety of resources and tools to help students search for part-time and summer jobs, internships and post-graduation opportunities, including the myFuture platform, and the Student Experience Record , with the goal of helping students become career-ready now and for their future.
delivery_context: Career services, events, advising, and online tools.
recommended_next_step: Review CaPS services and use the official advising route when personalized career support is needed.
source_evidence_excerpt: The Career Planning Service (CaPS), McGill’s central career services, supports student career and leadership development through career counselling and advising, skills development workshops, experiential learning, career exploration programs, mentoring, and career and networking events. CaPS provides and oversees a wide variety of resources and tools to help students search for part-time and summer jobs, internships and post-graduation opportunities, including the myFuture platform, and the Stu
limitation: Career support does not determine work authorization; international students should confirm permit rules separately.

service_name: International Student Work and Permits Guidance
intended_users: Full-time international McGill students seeking official guidance about working during their studies.
service_description: As a full-time International McGill student, you can work both on campus and off-campus, but it's important to ensure that you have the right documents to do so legally. “ Work ” is defined in the Immigration Canada Regulations as an activity for which wages are paid or commission is earned, OR that competes directly with activities of Canadian citizens or permanent residents in the Canadian labour market.
delivery_context: Online official guidance with links to work and permit topics.
recommended_next_step: Review the applicable work topic and confirm that the required documents and authorization are in place.
source_evidence_excerpt: As a full-time International McGill student, you can work both on campus and off-campus, but it's important to ensure that you have the right documents to do so legally.
limitation: The navigator must not interpret permit conditions or determine whether a student is legally authorized to work.

intended_users: McGill students looking for apartments, sublets, or roommates off campus, including newcomers arriving in Montreal.

contact_or_access_methods: Rental Information: https://www.mcgill.ca/students/housing/offcampus/rental-information | Apartment Listings: https://www.mcgill.ca/students/housing/offcampus/listings | Useful Information: https://www.mcgill.ca/students/housing/offcampus/useful-information
